Rituals have no power in and of themselves -- it's the will and imagination that move through them that gives them the power." When you have say a Catholic mass going and everyone there from the priest to the last parishioner knows every move by heart and are just going through the motions (eg mouthing all the right words and doing all the right actions but thinking about other things) is there still an effect there?

replies.title: Re: Various
replies.content:
1) Yes, and that's an important realization. The "other me" is your individuality, the real self you have from life to life, while the one with the bodies is your personality, the mask worn by the individuality during this incarnation.

2) The thing to keep in mind is that human beings are not the only ones with will and imagination. A Mass in the proper form (i.e., the pre-Vatican II Mass) has power even if done badly by the human participants because its form embodies an act of will and imagination on the part of transcendent powers. It's stronger when done with full intention by the participants, but it still has effect. The post-Vatican II Mass no longer has the correct form, and so it's empty unless the priest and congregation know how to fill it -- and by and large, they don't.

3) There's noting anti-Christian to it at all. The problem is simply that mainstream Christian churches don't want people making their own contacts with divinity -- they're supposed to be dependent on the church, after all.

replies.content: No, it's not the same thing. If you remember the discussion of the Cosmic Doctrine, the Individuality is the Cosmic atom, the part of the individual that is real on a Cosmic level and doesn't wholly belong to the solar system. replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
I think Rhydlyd covered it well. But here's my take:

A text is normally seen and experienced as a physical phenomena. However, the physical phenomena of a text is created by formulating a reflection of etheric / astral (or higher) phenomena; like all things, it is a confluence of an ascending and descending current. Awareness of this is the subject of the interactive approach.

A common approach is to build up meaning out of lower elements; letters form words, words form sentences, and a number of different meanings can be deduced from any particular word or sentence.

This process doesn't really go away, but it can be supplemented by a medial approach in which you make a connection to the astral impressions that created the text. At this level, the sentences are but reflections of a theme, the words are but reflections of parts of a sentence, and so on. The book is both necessary to the process (as above) but incidental to the point of it.

Finally, there is the interactive approach. The human mind is not closed and astral forms are not dead. Once there is a connection to higher forms, it is possible to experience them as not only the source of a text, but also an extant and living reality.

By the time one can see and experience such forms, it is also possible to rise to them...

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
My guess is that the religion of the future Russian great culture will evolve out of Russian Orthodoxy, but it won't be identical. The Magian religious vision is always that of a circle of reverent followers around a single, uniquely gifted individual who embodies the divine presence as fully as that can be present in the material world -- Jesus and his apostles, Muhammad and his companions, and so on. (Arthur surrounded by his knights is a reflection of that in the Magian pseudomorphosis of Europe -- and notice that Arthur, like Jesus and most other Magian holy figures, is expected to come again.)

Western Christianity reframes that in the standard Faustian mode, and Christ becomes the One who Teaches the Truth, the pathbreaker who overthrows the false teachings of the past and shows everyone the one true way to think and act. Believers don't form a notional circle around him, they follow him, cheering, on the grand march to infinity that is the template for the Faustian spirit.

Sobornost is different in another way. In Sobornost the circle is the reality, and Christ is the suffering servant, the archetype of the one who lays down his life for the community. Where the Magian vision focuses on the divine presence within the savior, and the Faustian vision focuses on the divine teaching, the vision of Sobornost focuses on the example of self-sacrifice. (And the vision of Tamanous will focus on Christ's forty days in the wilderness and his refusal to submit to community norms, because it's the vision of the individual in unique relationship to Spirit.)

replies.title: Re: Questions on Protection and Numerology
replies.content:
I've made quite a few of the red bag amulets without any sewing, try this:

1. Cut a square of red cloth about half the size of a large envelope out of a red tshirt (craft stores tend to have decent prices and selections if mallwart comes up empty).
2. Snip the corners off, making a rough circle.
3. Go around the edge of the circle, pinching a little fold of the cloth up with the tips of your scissors, and snipping through the fabric to make a crude hole. Line the outside edge with holes.
4. Take a long strand of good string and weave it in and out of the holes like a needle and thread. It helps to both start and end on the outside/underside of the bag.
5. Place your salt and nail in the bag, cinch it up and tie a knot with the two ends of string to keep it closed.
6. Adjust the length of the string, tie a square knot and snip off the excess ends of string and any excess bits of the red bag. Be careful you get the right end of the excess string, I've snipped right through my carefully sized work too many times.

Bam! Simple and cheap red bag amulet that can be made in 7 minutes.

comments.title: Oversoul
comments.content:
Hi JMG,
I am commenter Bird from your other Ecosophia site.
I've heard and read about an Oversoul that each of us have. This subject seems to be the same as a Guardian Angel. Can you briefly discuss the subject, or if it's too long, point me in the direction of a good book about that? My main question is: can we have any kind of contact with the Oversoul while here on earth?

Thanks again for all you do.

replies.title: Re: Oversoul
replies.content:
The term "oversoul" is used for several different things. In occult philosophy, each of us has an individuality or higher self, which is the core of consciousness that continues through all our incarnations, and is different from the personality or lower self, which lasts only for one life. The guardian angel is not the same thing -- you've got one of those, too, but it's not part of you, it's an emissary of the divine.

You can indeed have contact with your higher self, and with your guardian angel, while incarnate...but it takes a lot of work and a long slow process of spiritual development. Any book of standard occult training will get you started.

replies.content:
Obviously I'm not JMG, so please forgive me if this constitutes unwelcome commentary:

Working without a group is a perfectly acceptable way of doing things once you have the basics down. One of the things that makes the western tradition special is that the basics can even be learned out of a book. On the flip side, I'm not convinced that solitary practice is really a thing. You might not be practicing with other humans, but (I assume you are Christian) God and the angels are still very involved in the practice. If you aren't a hermit, then casual dealings with other people in daily life can be an excellent source of both feedback and spiritual practice.

With regard to the Golden dawn; I recognize the popular option is to tell people they are totally good enough for whatever goal they have. Let's try a different approach and assume that GD is for people who are in fact smarter and better than you are. Meditation and concentration are known for having the effect of making people more intelligent. If you are suitably motivated the first problem is fairly solvable. Moral refinement is an enormous topic, but the fact that it is open to everyone is a key teaching of Christianity. It isn't expressed in such terms, but I think the OSA work is a part of such a process. If you are suitable motivated the second problem is also fairly solvable.

If you don't want to become smarter and better before entering into the GD, perhaps there is some other system you are more comfortable with? You could work yourself out there and come back to it later. It sounds like you have the divination chops to direct your own studies. It may be that you want to study, pray, and meditate while you finish out your OSA work before coming back to GD.

I haven't done the OSA work, so this might demand an extra pinch of salt. In regards to enemies, you are not deluding yourself. Knowing that you are never separate from somebody acting in opposition to you is the first step. The sort of conduct you describe; without hostility or opposition, having no enemy, is the explicit goal of a number of martial arts. There are of course other ways to get there.

Hopefully this is of use to you.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Don't confuse working off karma with the notion, widespread in most faiths of the Piscean era, that suffering is virtuous. West: Njordh (Njord)
South: Freyja (Frige)
North: Frigga (Frig)
Below: Nerthus (Nerthus)
Above: Odin (Woden)

East: Odin (Woden)
West: Frey (Ing)
South: Freyja (Frige)
North: Frigga (Frig)
Below: Nerthus (Erce)
Above: Tyr (Tiw)

comments.title: The magical efficacy of freezing your tushie off
comments.content:
This is more in the nature of a comment than a question. In the past year or two occasional mentions have been made on this blog and on the other concerning the benefits of cold showers. It is said that exposure to cold water thoroughly cleanses the etheric body.

A few times I tried to turn down the temperature of my showers to the lower end of cool, but full-on cold I just couldn’t nerve myself to. But now I have crossed the Rubicon. For recently I discovered the teachings of Wim Hof, aka the Ice Man:

https://www.wimhofmethod.com/elearning

For the past week or so I have managed to take cold ones of increasing duration. The surprise is that after doing so you feel remarkably good, thoroughly invigorated. I’m doing this principally for my health, but also am benefiting from a nice clean aura, obnoxious to malefic spiritual entities, or so I am told.

The page I’ve linked offers a freebie mini-class you can scroll down to, which is how I got started. The part that helped me to overcome my, er, hesitancy was where Hof teaches that you can start by just thrusting a hand or a foot into the cold water stream, and gradually work your way up from there. Breathing exercises are also involved. I recommend it to anyone feeling sufficiently intrepid to give it a try.

replies.title: Re: The magical efficacy of freezing your tushie off
replies.content: Thanks for this. It's a grand old tradition, using cold water baths as an etheric cleansing, and I'm glad to see so many people taking it up.

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
Archdruid,

1) I recently lost my job and am embroiled in a minor court case with my employers. Would doing some planetary charity, burning incense to Jupiter and making a donation in his name be an appropriate way to pray for a good outcome?

2) When a planet is square another planet does it always portend negative outcomes? I'm thinking here of Uranus and Satrun. The two planets are supposed to be totally different, one favoring change and innovation, and the other favoring structure and stability. However, it seems to me that innovation is often a by product of structure, so their being square to one another might not necessarily mean something negative.

3) If I'm casting a spell and I've come up with a concept that plays an integral role in the intentionality of the spell, but there isn't a word to describe that concept, what happens if I simply make up a word to describe the concept?

Regards,

Syfen

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) Yes, that's a workable approach.

2) No. Squares are always difficult but the conflict they embody can be resolved constructively.

3) Yes, as long as you know exactly what it means.

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
On one of your podcasts I recently heard you say that as an occultist one can have a live that is close to normal but that is just on the outside isn't it? I had a "fear out of nowhere" watcher moment a few months ago and after some wrestling I think I moved on through that first pass. Now, what I am facing is that the world is so much more alive and full of gods, spirits and entities that are always there and I am facing internal resistance regarding the "far out" feeling. Somehow the whole planet has been put in a trance to not notice it and not until I took up magical practice I started to notice that what was pulling my attention actually exists and it looks much more insane to get back to it than to continue on the path.

Plunge without looking back? Embrace the "weird"? The process of realizing that there are not super humans but that being human is super is quite the trip and honestly I think I am going to focus now in managing the pitch of my intensity to remain steady instead of bursts of moments. I got my confirmation, it's real, it really is. Now, how do I get there and get the most out of it and not crash and burn in the process, since it is obvious now that mismanaged magic is not actually that hard.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) Yes, I meant that on the outside. You can live what looks to other people like a perfectly ordinary life -- well, except that you won't get caught up in popular crazes the way your neighbors do. On the inside? It's a much more interesting and vivid world.

2) That's why it helps to start by choosing an established system of magical training, follow its instructions systematically, and complete it. That'll give you the background you need to avoid the pitfalls and become a sane and successful mage.
